project.name: ScreenMind
project.one_liner: Privacy-first, 100% local screen-activity 'AI memory'. Captures screen -> understands with Gemma 4 E2B (via llama.cpp) -> searchable/chattable memory. Open-source alternative to Microsoft Recall.
project.model: Gemma 4 E2B — single model does vision + audio + reasoning. NOTE: it is 'Gemma 4 E2B' (user corrected: not Gemma 3; verified on web).

session_arc: 1. Full codebase read line-by-line to build complete mental model of the Python backend + frontend.
3. Pivoted to building a marketing/docs WEBSITE for the project (user has ~170 stars, wants a landing site). THIS IS THE ACTIVE WORK.
4. Side task: drafted a Reddit/community post about the project (see 'marketing_post').
5. Currently mid-website: polishing the 3D brain scroll experience.

bug_fixes_completed.note: These were already fixed and verified in the prior session. Listed so they are not 're-fixed' or regressed.
fixes: hotkey.py — 'logger used before definition': moved logger + config import above the keyboard try/except block.
agent_runner.py — unbound screenmind_sdk NameError: use fully-qualified screenmind.screenmind_sdk._set_current_agent(...); removed a redundant duplicate import.
screenmind_sdk.py — SDK POSTed to non-existent /api/capture: changed to /api/capture/bookmark (matches MCP server).
dependencies.py — removed dead GEMMA_MODELS list and unused hashlib import.
README.md — corrected ANALYSIS_MODE docs: default is 'fast', 3 options (fast/balanced/merged).
analysis_worker.py — pHash cache check moved BEFORE OCR so identical frames skip OCR entirely.

summary.py — productive_hours no longer always 0.0. Introduced shared _compute_day_metrics() helper used by BOTH the summary and standup endpoints (DRY, order-independent). Key details: uses (a.get('category') or 'other').lower() to avoid None.lower() crash; counts only coding/writing/terminal/design/meeting as productive (not browsing/communication); computes hours from actual timestamps with per-gap capping min(delta, 2*capture_interval) instead of count*interval; top_repos filled from dev_context JOIN (top 5 by frequency).

website.chosen_concept: The Brain (concept 02). User loved scrolling INSIDE the brain through neurons. Rejected the 'Foster'-style cards as ugly; wanted luminescent border on translucent text instead (implemented as .lumin / .tag CSS with text-stroke + glow).

how_the_site_works.hero: Camera starts OUTSIDE a small dense 'hero brain' (heroGroup, HCOUNT=4600, the pretty original look). It reacts to the cursor ('part the neurons').
how_the_site_works.scroll_narrative: body is 600vh. 4 text panels (data-a/data-b scroll ranges). Panel 1 = outside brain; panels 2-4 = INSIDE the brain flying through dense neurons. As scroll progresses, camera flies straight down -z INTO the big brain (group, COUNT=22000, fills the volume so interior is never empty), then wanders once inside.
how_the_site_works.big_brain: group/COUNT=22000 — dense volumetric brain you fly through. enterFade (progress 0.06->0.20) crossfades hero brain out and big interior brain in.
how_the_site_works.page_transition: Clicking a nav/doc link triggers go('doc', key): a WARP/hyperspace jump (travel streaks 'warp' LineSegments stretch like Star Wars light-speed / Interstellar wormhole) that flies to a CORNER inside the same big brain, then docs render there with dimmer/calmer neurons for readability. CORNER map defines start/end corners per page. Returning home = go('home').
how_the_site_works.docs_system: docs render from content.js SITE_CONTENT into #article + #docnav sidebar. Block renderer supports: h, h3, p, code, list, cards, steps, callout, youtube, img, loopvideo, stats. In-doc/sidebar nav uses swapDoc() = quick fade (NO warp); home<->doc uses go() = warp.
how_the_site_works.tech: Plain HTML + Three.js 0.160.1 from CDN (importmap). EffectComposer + UnrealBloom + OutputPass. NO build step.

design_decisions_and_user_preferences: User wants a PLAYFUL SANDBOX feel — fun interactive elements, cursor should push/part neurons and they spring back (damped physics).

The brain the user wants is specifically the one from the 3-prototype demo (landing/brain/). User repeatedly rejected versions where it 'felt like we exited the brain' — the target is being permanently surrounded by DENSE neurons on all sides (like the frame right after entering the brain).
Scroll should feel ENDLESS / infinite — like the wormhole travel in Interstellar — NOT a finite tunnel that ends, and NOT neurons that just get bigger as if approaching a wall.
Movement should not be purely straight; ideally drift up/down/left/right like moving through space-time made of neurons.
The brain is big: 'from afar it looks small but inside it's very big' — kept large so scrolling never hits a corner/edge.
Only the 1st screen-text is OUTSIDE the brain; screens 2/3/4 are INSIDE.
Separate/doc pages should land INSIDE the brain at a corner with LESS-dim, LESS-glowing neurons for good UX/readability (not a plain text page, not a starfield).
Transition preference: at peak speed neurons stretch and automatically look like hyperspace. Reuse the SAME brain from the earlier prototype for the interior.

Content strategy: put the most content in the 'scrolling inside the brain' section. All docs + features + working of ScreenMind must be reachable. Clicking things flies to a different corner of the brain (separate 'page').
Rejected ugly 'Foster' card style; prefers luminescent translucent text.
Marketing copy: user pushed back on product-pitch title, wanted to LEAD WITH A TECHNICAL ANGLE.

pending_next_tasks: DONE (2026-07-08): Restored hero-brain cursor interactivity. Root cause: production site used a fixed-distance ray projection (addScaledVector(ray.direction, 8)) that aimed at the wrong depth, so the 'part the neurons' effect missed the brain. Fixed by reverting to the original prototype's plane-intersection targeting (ray.intersectPlane(plane, pWorld) -> heroGroup.worldToLocal) and bumping radius 1.3->1.7 and force 0.06->0.075. User confirmed it now interacts.

known_gotchas: Some browsers block ES-module importmaps over file:// — serve with 'python -m http.server' from landing/ and open http://localhost:8000.

In index.html the old separate 'tube'/'cornerGroup' fields were RETIRED (set invisible) — docs now sit inside the big brain itself. Don't reintroduce a separate tunnel field; that caused the 'feels like we exited the brain' complaints.
Performance: heavy particle counts (22000 + 16000). The prior build notes call for prefers-reduced-motion + low-power fallbacks — not yet implemented.

marketing_post.context: User drafted a community/Reddit post about ScreenMind. Wanted it human-written, technical-first (not a product pitch), framed as 'posted ~a month ago, here's what changed since'.
key_points_to_convey: One Gemma 4 E2B via llama-server is the ONLY model; does all 3 jobs: screen vision->structured info, audio (voice memos + meeting transcription via E2B audio encoder, no Whisper), and chat/RAG over history + daily summaries.
Most effort went into OPTIMIZATION (background service on 4GB GTX 1650), not the AI part.

Chat pre-empts screen analysis: --parallel 1 single slot; sending a chat mid-analysis kills the in-flight request (closing HTTP conn frees the slot <1s), waits, answers; killed frame re-queued at front.

Perceptual-hash cache: pHash frame vs last for same app+window. Identical->reuse (no model call), small change->reuse layout quick pass, different->full pipeline. Chat apps go stale faster than editor.

'fast' mode: prefill empty <think></think> to skip reasoning tokens, OCR (easyocr) fed as text, screenshots shrunk to 768px, embeddings on MiniLM on CPU (never touch GPU), capture pauses on games/video editors.
Speed: ~12s/frame on 1650 (spills to RAM), ~3-4s on 3060 (fits in VRAM = the real jump), ~1s on 4090.
pip install screenmind; in-app model hub (no terminal); multi-model; Linux/Wayland; MCP for Claude/Cursor.
Privacy angle (user added explicitly): 100% local, zero network after model download, no telemetry, encrypted at rest, auto-redacts cards/api keys/passwords, incognito toggle, PIN lock.
Anticipate 'isn't this just Recall?' — difference: Recall/screenpipe dump raw OCR text; here the model actually READS the frame and OCR is just context.
marketing_post.next_side_effort_mentioned_by_user: Working on MULTI-MONITOR support for ScreenMind (asked community for input).

codebase_map.note: Backend is stable/understood. Package root is screenmind/. Key modules:
codebase_map.entry: screenmind/main.py — boots llama-server (setup_llama.py auto-download), DB, embedder, async Queue(maxsize=100), 3 workers, hotkeys, FastAPI (threaded), optional agent scheduler.

workers.capture_worker.py: smart ~5s poll loop, pHash dedup, privacy-zone + heavy-app auto-pause, inserts row immediately for instant timeline, extracts a11y text at capture time (correct-window fix), enqueues.
workers.analysis_worker.py: OCR -> Gemma -> layout -> dev-context -> embed -> store; per-app pHash 3-tier cache (check BEFORE OCR); chat pre-emption re-queue. (34KB)
workers.audio_worker.py: meeting detection, dual-channel record, chunked transcription, map-reduce summaries.

codebase_map.storage: database.py (SQLite WAL + FTS5 triggers + migrations), models.py (pydantic).
codebase_map.privacy: encryption.py (Fernet AES + OS keyring), data_filter.py (redaction: CC Luhn, SSN, API keys, JWT, passwords).
codebase_map.api: server.py (FastAPI + PIN-lock middleware), dependencies.py (shared state), routes/ (16 modules incl. summary.py), static/ (vanilla-JS dashboard).
codebase_map.platform_support: windows.py (Win32+UIA), macos.py (AppKit+AXUIElement), linux.py (X11 + Wayland via grim/IPC/XDG Portal).
codebase_map.capture: screen.py (mss/Wayland facade), wayland.py, window.py, dedup.py (pHash, threshold 8 hamming), hotkey.py, voice_recorder.py.
codebase_map.other: mcp_server.py (MCP for Claude/Cursor/VSCode, read-only), screenmind_sdk.py (Python plugin SDK), config.py (pydantic settings), setup_llama.py (auto-detect+install llama-server), default_agents/ (4 built-in .md agents).
codebase_map.tests: tests/ — 25+ pytest modules. CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml) runs lightweight deps (requirements-test.txt, no torch).

cleanup_note: Temp helper scripts (_extract.py, _analyze.py, _probe.py, _probe2.py, _prompts.py, _peek.ps1, _narrative.json, _prompts.json) were created in the project root to build this. _narrative.json and _prompts.json are worth keeping for recall; the .py/.ps1 scripts can be deleted.
